candidates.name: Jordan Lee
candidates.short_bio: Jordan Lee is a former public school teacher and community organizer running for Congress on a platform focused on climate action, affordable healthcare, and strengthening democracy.

candidates.name: Pat Smith
candidates.short_bio: Pat Smith is a two-term state legislator emphasizing tax cuts and deregulation, and is the leading opponent in the race.

candidates.name: Riley Chen
candidates.short_bio: Riley Chen is a tech entrepreneur focused on government modernization and innovation, running as a centrist alternative.

candidates.name: Morgan Patel
candidates.short_bio: Morgan Patel is a small business owner and housing advocate joining the ticket to champion economic opportunity and affordable housing.

issues.title: Climate Action & Clean Energy
issues.summary: Jordan Lee’s plan to cut carbon pollution, create clean energy jobs, and make communities more resilient to climate disasters.
issues.body:
Our district is already feeling the effects of the climate crisis—from flooded basements and washed-out roads to extreme heat that endangers seniors and outdoor workers. Jordan Lee’s climate action plan sets ambitious but achievable goals to cut carbon pollution, invest in clean energy, and help families save money on their utility bills.

The plan centers on three pillars: (1) 100% clean electricity by 2035 through investments in solar, wind, and energy storage; (2) resilient communities, including modernized stormwater systems, green infrastructure, and emergency preparedness; and (3) good-paying union jobs in the clean energy transition, with a focus on retraining workers from legacy industries. Jordan will fight to end taxpayer subsidies for the biggest polluters and redirect those funds to local clean energy and resilience projects.

This policy is rooted in environmental justice: communities of color and low-income neighborhoods have borne the brunt of pollution for decades. Jordan’s plan ensures those communities are first in line for clean air, clean water, and climate investments.

issues.title: Protecting Our Environment & Open Spaces
issues.summary: Safeguarding clean air and water, preserving parks and farms, and cleaning up toxic sites in our communities.
issues.body:
Jordan Lee believes every family deserves clean air, safe drinking water, and access to green spaces. As our region grows, we must balance development with strong protections for our rivers, forests, and farmland. Jordan supports fully funding the Environmental Protection Agency, strengthening enforcement against corporate polluters, and expanding federal support for local land conservation.

The plan includes targeted investments to replace lead pipes, clean up legacy toxic sites, and expand urban tree canopies that reduce heat and improve air quality. Jordan will also support farmers who practice sustainable agriculture and help them adapt to changing weather patterns, so we can secure our local food supply while protecting the land.

By partnering with local governments, tribal nations, and community organizations, Jordan’s environmental agenda will protect the places we love for future generations.

issues.title: Affordable Healthcare for All
issues.summary: Lowering costs, protecting coverage for preexisting conditions, and expanding mental health and reproductive care.
issues.body:
No one should have to choose between filling a prescription and paying the rent. Jordan Lee supports a universal, affordable healthcare system that guarantees coverage regardless of income, employment, or preexisting conditions. That starts with allowing Medicare to negotiate drug prices, expanding subsidies on the Affordable Care Act marketplaces, and creating a robust public option that anyone can buy into.

Jordan’s plan also prioritizes mental health and addiction services by increasing reimbursement rates for counselors and investing in community-based treatment, especially in rural and underserved areas. Reproductive healthcare, including access to contraception and abortion, must remain legal and accessible. Jordan will co-sponsor legislation to codify Roe v. Wade protections into federal law.

By investing in preventive care and community health, we can improve health outcomes while reducing long-term costs.

issues.title: Public Education & Student Success
issues.summary: Fully funding public schools, supporting educators, and expanding early childhood education and debt-free college options.
issues.body:
As a former public school teacher, Jordan Lee has seen firsthand how underfunding and overcrowded classrooms hold students back. Jordan’s education plan would increase federal support for K–12 schools in low-income districts, raise teacher pay, and ensure every classroom has up-to-date textbooks, technology, and safe facilities.

The plan calls for universal pre-K, expanded after-school programs, and mental health supports so students can thrive. For higher education, Jordan supports making community college tuition-free and significantly reducing student loan burdens by expanding income-based repayment and targeted loan forgiveness for public service workers.

Jordan will also invest in career and technical education, apprenticeships, and partnerships with local employers so that every student—whether college-bound or not—has a path to a good job.

issues.title: Good Jobs & a Local Economy That Works for Everyone
issues.summary: Raising wages, supporting small businesses, and investing in modern infrastructure to create good-paying jobs.
issues.body:
Too many families in our district are working harder than ever but still falling behind. Jordan Lee’s economic plan focuses on raising wages, lowering costs, and investing in local businesses. That includes raising the federal minimum wage, strengthening collective bargaining rights, and expanding access to affordable childcare so parents can work.

Jordan will fight for targeted tax credits and low-interest loans for small businesses, especially those owned by women, veterans, and people of color. By upgrading roads, bridges, broadband, and clean energy infrastructure, we can create thousands of good-paying union jobs while making our region more competitive.

The plan also addresses the rising cost of housing through incentives for affordable housing development and protections against predatory landlords.

issues.title: Safe Communities & Smart Public Safety
issues.summary: Investing in community safety, gun violence prevention, and accountability in law enforcement.
issues.body:
Every family deserves to feel safe in their home, on their block, and at their school. Jordan Lee believes in a both-and approach to public safety: smart, evidence-based policing alongside investments in housing, mental health, and youth programs.

Jordan supports universal background checks, red-flag laws, and banning assault weapons and high-capacity magazines. At the same time, Jordan backs increased funding for community violence interruption programs, mobile crisis response teams, and reentry services that reduce recidivism.

Law enforcement must be accountable to the communities they serve. Jordan will support national standards for use of force, body camera policies, and independent investigations of police misconduct.

issues.title: Immigration Reform with Dignity
issues.summary: A humane immigration system that keeps families together, secures our borders, and offers a path to citizenship.
issues.body:
Our immigration system is broken, but cruelty is not a solution. Jordan Lee supports comprehensive immigration reform that provides an earned path to citizenship for long-time residents, protects Dreamers, and modernizes our legal immigration system.

Jordan will back smart, accountable border security that focuses on stopping drug and human trafficking while respecting human rights and international law. The plan also includes expanded support for asylum processing and legal assistance so cases can be resolved fairly and efficiently.

Families belong together. Jordan opposes policies that separate children from their parents and will fight to end for-profit immigration detention.

issues.title: Justice System Reform
issues.summary: Ending mass incarceration, eliminating discriminatory sentencing, and expanding reentry and restorative justice programs.
issues.body:
The United States locks up more people than any other nation, often for nonviolent offenses tied to poverty and addiction. Jordan Lee supports ending mandatory minimums for nonviolent drug offenses, eliminating cash bail for low-level charges, and investing in diversion and treatment instead of incarceration.

Jordan will push for sentencing reforms that address racial disparities and create a path for resentencing people serving outdated or excessive sentences. The plan also includes expanded funding for public defenders and reentry programs that provide housing, job training, and mental health care.

By focusing on accountability, rehabilitation, and prevention, we can build a justice system that truly keeps communities safe and reflects our values.

issues.title: Voting Rights & a Strong Democracy
issues.summary: Protecting the right to vote, ending partisan gerrymandering, and getting big money out of politics.
issues.body:
Our democracy works best when every eligible voter can participate and every vote is counted. Jordan Lee supports restoring and strengthening the Voting Rights Act, expanding early voting and vote-by-mail options, and making Election Day a federal holiday.

Jordan will co-sponsor legislation to end partisan gerrymandering by requiring independent redistricting commissions and to increase transparency in campaign finance. The plan includes strong disclosure rules for dark money and public financing options that amplify small-dollar donations.

Defending democracy is not a partisan issue—it’s a patriotic one. Jordan will oppose any attempt to overturn certified election results or intimidate election workers.

issues.title: Infrastructure & Transportation
issues.summary: Fixing roads and bridges, expanding transit, and building climate-resilient infrastructure across the district.
issues.body:
From pothole-ridden streets to aging bridges, our infrastructure is overdue for serious investment. Jordan Lee’s infrastructure plan would prioritize fixing what’s broken while building for a climate-resilient future. That includes modernizing water and sewer systems, expanding high-speed broadband to every home, and upgrading the electric grid.

Transportation is central to opportunity. Jordan supports expanded bus and rail service, safer bike lanes, and complete streets that work for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. The plan also calls for electric vehicle charging infrastructure and incentives to help families and small businesses transition to cleaner transportation.

These projects will create good-paying jobs, reduce traffic and pollution, and make it easier for everyone to get where they need to go.

media_items.title: State of the District Address 2023
media_items.description: Jordan Lee’s comprehensive 2023 speech outlining a vision for climate action, healthcare, education, and democracy reforms.
media_items.transcript: Thank you all for being here tonight. Our district is at a crossroads. In the year ahead, we can choose bold climate action, truly affordable healthcare, world-class public schools, and a democracy that works for everyone—not just the wealthy and well-connected...

media_items.title: Education Town Hall Highlights 2023
media_items.description: Highlights from Jordan’s 2023 education town hall with students, parents, and teachers.
media_items.transcript: In every classroom I’ve visited this year, I’ve met students full of potential and educators stretched too thin. Tonight we talked about fully funding our schools, expanding mental health supports, and making sure zip code no longer determines opportunity...

media_items.title: Remarks on the Healthcare Plan 2023
media_items.description: A 2023 speech where Jordan details the plan to lower costs and expand healthcare access.
media_items.transcript: When I talk to families in our district, healthcare costs are the number one concern. No one should be bankrupted by an illness or injury. That’s why our plan caps out-of-pocket costs, lets Medicare negotiate drug prices, and expands coverage in rural areas...

media_items.title: Climate Action Rally Clip 2023
media_items.description: A short clip from the 2023 climate action rally where Jordan speaks about clean energy jobs.
media_items.transcript: We don’t have to choose between good jobs and a livable planet. With the right investments, we can build wind turbines, install solar panels, and retrofit buildings right here in our own communities...

media_items.title: Local News Interview 2023
media_items.description: Jordan’s 2023 interview on local news discussing voting rights and democracy reforms.
media_items.transcript: In this interview, Jordan explains why protecting the right to vote and ending partisan gerrymandering are essential to rebuilding trust in our democracy...

media_items.title: Campaign Launch Speech 2024
media_items.description: The official 2024 campaign launch speech outlining Jordan’s vision for the district.
media_items.transcript: Today we launch a campaign powered by neighbors, not special interests. Together, we will fight for climate action, affordable healthcare, great schools, and a democracy that represents all of us...

media_items.title: First TV Ad: "Neighbors"
media_items.description: The first 2024 television ad introducing Jordan Lee to voters across the district.
media_items.transcript: Narrator: In our community, neighbors look out for each other. That’s how Jordan Lee has always led—organizing for better schools, cleaner air, and more good-paying jobs...

media_items.title: Radio Interview on Economic Plan
media_items.description: A radio interview where Jordan discusses plans for good jobs and small business support.
media_items.transcript: On today’s show, Jordan walks through a plan to raise wages, support small businesses, and rebuild local infrastructure while keeping our communities affordable...

media_items.title: Debate Night Clip: Healthcare Contrast
media_items.description: A highlight from the 2025 debate where Jordan contrasts their healthcare plan with opponent Smith.
media_items.transcript: Moderator: How does your healthcare plan differ from your opponent’s? Jordan: The difference is simple. I believe healthcare is a right, not a privilege. My plan expands coverage and lowers costs, while my opponent would raise premiums and cut protections for preexisting conditions...

media_items.title: Virtual Town Hall: Healthcare & Prescription Costs
media_items.description: A virtual town hall clip featuring questions from voters about healthcare and prescription drug prices.
media_items.transcript: Caller: My family’s premiums have doubled in five years. What will you do to bring costs down? Jordan: First, we’ll let Medicare negotiate directly with drug companies. Second, we’ll cap out-of-pocket costs and create a public option so families can choose an affordable plan...

media_items.title: Youth Roundtable on Education 2025
media_items.description: A 2025 roundtable speech with high school and college students about education, mental health, and debt.
media_items.transcript: Sitting in a circle with students from across the district, Jordan listens as they describe the pressure they feel—from student debt, from high-stakes testing, and from untreated anxiety and depression...

media_items.title: Web Exclusive Message to Supporters 2026
media_items.description: A short web-only video updating supporters on the state of the campaign in early 2026.
media_items.transcript: Thanks to your support, we’ve built a grassroots campaign that is knocking on doors, calling neighbors, and sharing our vision for the district. In the months ahead, we’ll need you more than ever...

blog_posts.title: Investing in Our Public Schools in 2026
blog_posts.excerpt: Our 2026 education agenda doubles down on fully funded public schools, better pay for educators, and the support students need to thrive.
blog_posts.body:
As a former public school teacher, I know that every budget line is a decision about what kind of future we are willing to build. In 2026, our agenda for public schools is focused on one core idea: every child, in every zip code, deserves an excellent, well-resourced education.

That means increasing federal support for high-need districts so that schools in low-income neighborhoods are no longer held back by weak local tax bases. It means raising teacher pay so talented educators can afford to stay in the classroom, and providing funds for updated textbooks, science labs, and secure, modern facilities.

We are also investing in school-based mental health supports, after-school enrichment, and partnerships with community organizations. These investments do more than raise test scores—they keep young people safe, engaged, and on track to succeed. In the months ahead, I will continue visiting classrooms across our district to make sure this plan reflects what students, parents, and educators say they need most.

blog_posts.title: Student Mental Health and the Need for More School Counselors
blog_posts.excerpt: Young people are facing record levels of stress and anxiety. It is time we match their courage with real investments in mental health support at school.
blog_posts.body:
In conversations with students across our district, I hear the same message again and again: they are under immense pressure, and too many are shouldering it alone. We cannot talk honestly about education without talking about student mental health.

Our plan calls for increasing the number of school counselors, social workers, and psychologists so that every school meets recommended staffing ratios. That means federal funding targeted to districts that agree to hire and retain these professionals, especially in high-need communities.

We also need to reduce stigma by integrating social-emotional learning into the school day and giving educators the training they need to recognize warning signs. When students know there are trusted adults they can turn to, they are more likely to ask for help before a crisis.

Investing in mental health is not a luxury—it is essential to helping students learn, stay safe, and build the resilience they will carry throughout their lives.

blog_posts.title: Community Colleges and Career Training for the Jobs of Tomorrow
blog_posts.excerpt: By investing in community colleges and technical training, we can open doors to good-paying jobs without forcing students into decades of debt.
blog_posts.body:
Not every student wants or needs a four-year degree, but every student deserves a clear path to a good job. Community colleges and technical training programs are at the heart of our workforce strategy.

Jordans plan would make community college tuition-free for most families, expand federal support for apprenticeship programs, and incentivize partnerships between schools and local employers. That means curricula designed with actual jobs in mind, from clean energy installation and advanced manufacturing to healthcare and IT.

For adult learners, we propose flexible scheduling, childcare support, and wraparound services that make it possible to retrain while working and caring for a family. When we invest in career pathways close to home, we keep talent in our communities and help local businesses grow.

This is how we build an economy where opportunity does not depend on your parents income or whether you can afford a four-year campus far from home.

blog_posts.title: What I Learned as a Teacher
blog_posts.excerpt: Before running for Congress, I spent years in the classroom. Those experiences shape every part of my education agenda.
blog_posts.body:
In my first year as a public school teacher, I learned quickly that policy debates in Washington feel very far away when you are standing in front of thirty students who all need your attention at once. I saw brilliant minds held back by outdated textbooks, overcrowded classrooms, and the stress their families carried from low wages and unstable housing.

Those years taught me that educators are doing heroic work in a system that too often sets them up to fail. It also showed me what is possible when schools have the resources they need: smaller class sizes, collaborative planning time, and wraparound services that support the whole child.

The policies I am fighting for in Congress—fully funded schools, better pay for teachers and support staff, expanded mental health services—are rooted in that lived experience. When I say I will listen to educators and students, it is because I have been in their shoes.

blog_posts.title: Clean Energy Jobs Are Coming to Our District
blog_posts.excerpt: New investments in solar, wind, and building retrofits will create hundreds of good-paying jobs right here at home.
blog_posts.body:
Thanks to recent federal legislation and state partnerships, our district is poised to become a regional leader in clean energy. Over the next few years, we could see hundreds of new jobs in solar installation, wind turbine maintenance, and energy-efficient building retrofits.

Jordans climate action plan is designed to make sure those jobs go first to local workers. That means funding apprenticeship programs, partnering with unions and community colleges, and ensuring fair wages and safe working conditions.

We are also prioritizing projects that reduce utility bills for low- and middle-income households, like weatherization and community solar. Clean energy is not just about reducing pollution—it is about building a more affordable, resilient local economy.

If we get this right, our district can prove that climate action and economic opportunity go hand in hand.

blog_posts.title: Preparing for a Hotter Summer: Climate Resilience in Our Neighborhoods
blog_posts.excerpt: Extreme heat is becoming the new normal. Here is how Jordans climate plan will help keep our communities safe.
blog_posts.body:
The last few summers have brought record-breaking heat to our district, with dangerous consequences for seniors, outdoor workers, and families without reliable air conditioning. Climate change is not a distant threat—it is already reshaping daily life.

Jordans climate resilience agenda includes funding for cooling centers, expanded tree canopy in urban heat islands, and modernized stormwater systems that prevent flooded basements when heavy rains hit. We will push for federal grants that allow local governments to upgrade infrastructure before disasters strike, not after.

The plan also supports home weatherization and energy-efficiency upgrades that lower utility bills while keeping homes cooler. These investments will be targeted first to communities that have borne the brunt of pollution and disinvestment for decades.

By acting now, we can save lives, protect property, and build neighborhoods that are ready for the hotter summers we know are coming.

blog_posts.title: Environmental Justice and the Fight for Clean Air
blog_posts.excerpt: No child should grow up next to a smokestack or a toxic dump. Environmental justice is at the heart of our clean air agenda.
blog_posts.body:
In too many neighborhoods, children grow up breathing air that would never be tolerated in wealthier zip codes. Asthma rates are higher, emergency room visits are more frequent, and families live with the constant fear that the water coming from their tap is not safe.

Environmental justice is not a slogan—it is a commitment to fixing this unequal burden of pollution. My plan directs federal resources to replace lead pipes, clean up legacy toxic sites, and enforce clean air and water standards in communities that have been ignored for decades.

We will also strengthen monitoring and transparency so residents can see exactly what is in the air they breathe, and hold polluters accountable when they break the rules. Clean air and clean water are basic rights, not privileges reserved for the wealthy.

When we center environmental justice, we improve public health, create jobs in cleanup and remediation, and honor the communities that have been fighting for these changes for years.

blog_posts.title: How Our Healthcare Plan Lowers Prescription Costs
blog_posts.excerpt: Families in our district are paying too much at the pharmacy counter. Here is how Jordans plan brings those costs down.
blog_posts.body:
When we ask voters what keeps them up at night, prescription drug costs are always near the top of the list. Too many people are skipping doses, splitting pills, or leaving life-saving medications unfilled because they simply cannot afford them.

Jordans healthcare plan tackles this head-on by allowing Medicare to negotiate directly with drug companies and by limiting annual out-of-pocket costs for patients. The plan also cracks down on price-gouging and pay-for-delay schemes that keep cheaper generics off the market.

For people with chronic conditions like diabetes, asthma, or heart disease, predictable and affordable medication costs are the difference between stability and crisis. By reining in corporate greed and using the power of negotiation, we can make sure no one in our district has to choose between their health and their groceries.

blog_posts.title: Town Hall Recap: Healthcare and Hospital Closures
blog_posts.excerpt: Residents packed the auditorium to talk about hospital closures, rising premiums, and what real healthcare security looks like.
blog_posts.body:
At our recent healthcare town hall, we heard from nurses, patients, and hospital workers who are living with the consequences of repeated hospital closures in our region. People described driving hours for emergency care, losing maternity wards, and watching neighbors delay treatment because the nearest clinic shut down.

Jordan shared a plan to stabilize community hospitals by increasing federal support for rural and safety-net facilities, raising Medicaid reimbursement rates, and incentivizing partnerships that keep local clinics open. The plan also expands telehealth in areas where travel is difficult, while insisting that virtual care complement, not replace, in-person services.

Several attendees asked pointed questions about how we will pay for these improvements. Jordan explained that by allowing Medicare to negotiate drug prices and closing tax loopholes for the wealthiest corporations, we can invest in accessible, affordable care without cutting benefits.

blog_posts.title: Nurses Association Endorses Jordan Lee
blog_posts.excerpt: Citing a strong commitment to affordable healthcare and safe staffing levels, the regional Nurses Association has endorsed Jordan Lee.
blog_posts.body:
Today, the Mid-State Nurses Association announced its endorsement of Jordan Lee for Congress. In their statement, they praised Jordans detailed plan to lower prescription drug costs, protect coverage for people with preexisting conditions, and ensure safe staffing levels in hospitals and clinics.

Nurses are on the front lines of our healthcare system, and when they speak, we should listen, Jordan said at the press conference. Their endorsement is a powerful vote of confidence in our vision for affordable, high-quality care for every family.

Jordans healthcare agenda includes raising reimbursement rates for nurses in community health settings, expanding loan forgiveness for nurses who practice in rural or underserved areas, and investing in workplace safety to prevent burnout and injury.

This endorsement adds to a growing coalition of healthcare professionals, patient advocates, and community organizations backing Jordans campaign.

blog_posts.title: Small Businesses and a Local Economy That Works for Everyone
blog_posts.excerpt: From neighborhood diners to start-ups on Main Street, small businesses keep our communities strong. Jordans plan gives them the support they deserve.
blog_posts.body:
Small businesses are more than storefronts—they are the places where neighbors meet, young people get their first jobs, and communities build wealth. But too many local entrepreneurs are struggling with rising rents, limited access to credit, and competition from giant corporations.

Jordans economic plan includes targeted tax credits for small businesses that create good-paying local jobs, as well as low-interest loan programs for entrepreneurs who have been historically shut out of traditional financing. We will prioritize businesses owned by women, veterans, and people of color.

By investing in main streets across our district, upgrading infrastructure, and expanding high-speed broadband, we can help small businesses grow while keeping our communities affordable and livable.

blog_posts.title: Keeping Our Elections Safe and Accessible
blog_posts.excerpt: The right to vote is the foundation of our democracy. We must protect it and make it easier, not harder, for people to participate.
blog_posts.body:
Around the country, we have seen a wave of laws that make it harder to vote—from cutting early voting hours to closing polling places in certain neighborhoods. At the same time, foreign interference and election misinformation remain real threats.

My plan for voting rights and democracy takes a comprehensive approach: restoring and strengthening the Voting Rights Act, setting national standards for early voting and vote-by-mail, and investing in secure voting infrastructure that is resilient against cyberattacks.

We also need to end partisan gerrymandering by requiring independent redistricting commissions and increasing transparency in campaign finance so voters know who is trying to influence their choices.

A healthy democracy depends on participation. Our goal should be simple: every eligible voter can cast a ballot easily and have confidence that it will be counted.

blog_posts.title: Launching Our Volunteer Door-Knocking Team
blog_posts.excerpt: Face-to-face conversations are the heart of this campaign. Here is how you can join our new door-knocking team.
blog_posts.body:
When neighbors talk to neighbors, we win—not just elections, but long-term trust. That is why we are launching a volunteer door-knocking program that will reach thousands of households across the district.

Volunteers will receive training on how to have respectful, issue-focused conversations, along with scripts and materials that answer common questions about Jordans plans on climate, healthcare, education, and more. You do not need prior experience—just a willingness to listen and share your story.

Sign up on our Volunteer page to choose your preferred neighborhoods and times. Whether you can give one hour a week or ten, there is a role for you in this effort to build a people-powered campaign.

blog_posts.title: Our Plan to Prevent Gun Violence and Keep Communities Safe
blog_posts.excerpt: We do not have to accept gun violence as inevitable. Here is a practical, evidence-based plan to keep our communities safe.
blog_posts.body:
Parents should not have to worry that sending their kids to school means sending them into harms way. We can honor the Second Amendment while taking common-sense steps to prevent gun violence and save lives.

My plan includes universal background checks, red-flag laws that temporarily remove firearms from people who pose a danger to themselves or others, and a ban on assault weapons and high-capacity magazines that were designed for the battlefield, not our streets.

We will also invest in community violence interruption programs, youth employment initiatives, and mental health services that address the root causes of violence. Public safety is about more than policing—it is about building strong, stable communities where everyone has what they need to thrive.

blog_posts.title: Why Im Running for Congress
blog_posts.excerpt: From the classroom to the campaign trail, this is the story of why I decided to run—and what we can change together.
blog_posts.body:
For years, I watched students work hard and dream big, only to run into barriers that no young person should have to face: medical bills that wiped out their families savings, crushing student debt, and jobs that did not pay enough to cover rent. I saw neighborhoods flooded by storms made worse by climate change and voters standing in line for hours just to cast a ballot.

I decided to run for Congress because I believe our government should work for the people who are doing everything right and still feel like the deck is stacked against them. I am running to fight for climate action, affordable healthcare, great public schools, and a democracy where every voice matters.

This campaign is about more than one election—it is about building a movement that brings neighbors together to demand better. I hope you will join us.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan Lee strongly supports transitioning to 100% clean electricity by 2035 while creating good-paying union jobs and prioritizing environmental justice.
key_points: Set a national goal of 100% clean electricity by 2035 with major investments in solar, wind, and energy storage.
End taxpayer subsidies for the largest polluters and redirect funds to local clean energy and resilience projects.
Guarantee that communities most impacted by pollution are first in line for climate investments and clean energy jobs.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee views the climate crisis as an urgent threat and a major economic opportunity. Jordans plan centers on three pillars: achieving 100% clean electricity by 2035, building resilient communities that can withstand extreme weather, and creating good-paying union jobs in the clean energy economy. The proposal includes ending subsidies for fossil fuel corporations and redirecting those resources to community-based clean energy and infrastructure upgrades, with a particular focus on neighborhoods that have faced the highest pollution burdens.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes Jordans aggressive clean energy timeline and favors expanding fossil fuel production alongside limited incentives for renewables.
key_points: Supports continued expansion of domestic oil and gas production and new pipeline infrastructure.
Opposes deadlines for phasing out fossil fuels, calling them unrealistic and harmful to businesses.
Backs modest tax credits for renewables but rejects major federal climate investments.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith argues that rapid climate mandates will hurt the economy and raise energy prices. Smith supports an all-of-the-above energy strategy that keeps fossil fuels at the center of the energy mix, alongside limited incentives for renewables. Smith has criticized Jordans 100% clean electricity goal as reckless and instead calls for loosening regulations on drilling and pipeline construction.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan strongly supports protecting clean air and water, preserving parks and farms, and cleaning up toxic sites in overburdened communities.
key_points: Fully fund environmental enforcement to hold corporate polluters accountable.
Invest in replacing lead pipes and cleaning up legacy toxic sites.
Support sustainable agriculture and conservation of parks, forests, and farmland.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee believes environmental policy should start with the basics: clean air, safe drinking water, and access to green spaces. Jordan supports fully funding the EPA, strengthening pollution enforcement, and directing federal dollars to replace lead pipes and remediate contaminated sites. The plan also includes expanding conservation efforts for parks and farmland and supporting farmers who adopt sustainable practices.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith is skeptical of stronger environmental regulations and prioritizes reducing compliance costs for industry over expanding enforcement.
key_points: Supports streamlining environmental permitting to speed up development projects.
Opposes new federal regulations on air and water pollution, favoring voluntary measures.
Backs conservation of some open spaces but opposes expanding protected areas that limit drilling or logging.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith argues that environmental regulations have become too burdensome for businesses and local governments. Smith favors easing permitting rules for pipelines, factories, and large developments, and has opposed proposals to strengthen federal air and water standards. While Smith supports some conservation initiatives, Smith frequently votes against efforts to expand protected lands that could limit resource extraction.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan strongly supports a universal, affordable healthcare system with a robust public option and lower drug prices.
key_points: Allow Medicare to negotiate prescription drug prices and cap out-of-pocket costs.
Create a public option that anyone can buy into, regardless of employment status.
Expand mental health, addiction treatment, and reproductive healthcare services.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee believes healthcare is a human right. Jordan supports building toward universal, affordable coverage by letting Medicare negotiate drug prices, capping out-of-pocket costs, and creating a robust public option available to anyone. The plan strengthens the Affordable Care Act, expands subsidies, and invests heavily in mental health, addiction treatment, and reproductive healthcare, including protections for abortion access.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes a public option and supports rolling back parts of the Affordable Care Act in favor of market-based reforms.
key_points: Opposes government-run or public option plans, calling them a step toward socialized medicine.
Supports expanding health savings accounts and short-term insurance plans.
Backs repealing regulations Smith argues drive up premiums, including some ACA consumer protections.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith argues that government involvement is the main problem in healthcare. Smith opposes Jordans public option proposal and supports rolling back major parts of the Affordable Care Act, favoring health savings accounts, association health plans, and short-term insurance. Smith has criticized drug price negotiation as government price fixing and instead favors voluntary industry agreements.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan strongly supports fully funding public schools, raising teacher pay, and expanding early childhood and higher education opportunities.
key_points: Increase federal funding for high-need K–12 districts and raise teacher salaries.
Provide universal pre-K and expand after-school and mental health programs.
Make community college tuition-free and reduce student debt burdens.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Drawing on years of experience as a teacher, Jordan Lees education plan increases federal support for underfunded schools, raises teacher pay, and ensures every classroom has modern materials and safe facilities. The plan includes universal pre-K, expanded after-school and counseling services, and major investments in community colleges, apprenticeships, and debt relief for public service workers.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith favors school vouchers and privatization over increased federal funding for public schools.
key_points: Supports redirecting public funds to private and charter schools through voucher programs.
Opposes significant increases in federal education spending.
Backs tying school funding to standardized test performance.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith argues that competition, not funding, is the key to improving schools. Smith supports expanded voucher programs that move public dollars to private and charter schools and opposes large federal investments in traditional public schools. Smith favors high-stakes testing and merit pay, while criticizing proposals for universal pre-K and tuition-free community college as too costly.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan supports raising wages, strengthening unions, and investing in small businesses and infrastructure.
key_points: Raise the federal minimum wage and protect collective bargaining rights.
Provide tax credits and low-interest loans for small businesses.
Invest in infrastructure and affordable housing to create jobs and lower costs.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lees economic agenda focuses on building a local economy that works for working families, not just corporations. The plan calls for raising the federal minimum wage, protecting the right to organize, and expanding tax credits and financing options for small businesses. Jordan also supports major investments in infrastructure and affordable housing to create jobs and reduce cost-of-living pressures.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes minimum wage increases and union protections, focusing instead on corporate tax cuts and deregulation.
key_points: Opposes raising the federal minimum wage, emphasizing market-set wages.
Supports corporate tax cuts and rolling back labor regulations.
Criticizes large federal infrastructure and housing investments as excessive spending.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smiths economic approach centers on tax cuts and deregulation. Smith opposes raising the minimum wage or expanding union protections, arguing these measures hurt business growth. Instead, Smith promotes corporate tax reductions and reduced federal oversight, while criticizing Jordans infrastructure and housing investments as fiscally irresponsible.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan supports a both-and approach to safety: gun violence prevention, accountability in policing, and investments in housing and mental health.
key_points: Enact universal background checks and red-flag laws and ban assault weapons.
Fund community violence interruption and mobile crisis response teams.
Establish national standards for use of force, body cameras, and independent investigations.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee believes real safety comes from strong communities and smart, accountable public safety policies. Jordan supports universal background checks, red-flag laws, and bans on assault weapons, while also investing in community-based violence interruption, mental health crisis teams, and reentry support. The plan calls for national standards on police use of force and independent investigations of misconduct to build trust.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes new gun safety laws and emphasizes increasing penalties and expanding law enforcement powers.
key_points: Opposes bans on assault weapons and universal background check expansions.
Supports mandatory minimum sentences and increased funding for traditional policing.
Rejects national standards for use of force as federal overreach.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith frames public safety primarily through a law-and-order lens. Smith opposes most new gun safety measures, including assault weapons bans and expanded background checks, and instead supports tougher sentencing and expanded law enforcement powers. Smith has criticized proposals for national policing standards as undue federal interference in local control.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan supports comprehensive immigration reform with an earned path to citizenship, protections for Dreamers, and humane border policies.
key_points: Create an earned path to citizenship for long-time residents and essential workers.
Protect Dreamers and modernize legal immigration pathways.
Implement accountable, humane border security and end family separation policies.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee supports a comprehensive overhaul of the immigration system that keeps families together and recognizes the contributions of immigrants to our communities. Jordan backs an earned path to citizenship for long-time residents, strong protections for Dreamers, and modernized legal immigration channels. Jordan also supports accountable border security that focuses on real threats while ending cruel practices like family separation and for-profit detention.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes a path to citizenship for most undocumented immigrants and supports expanding detention and border wall construction.
key_points: Opposes broad legalization or citizenship pathways, favoring limited temporary status at most.
Supports expanding physical barriers and detention facilities at the border.
Backs policies that make it harder to claim asylum in the United States.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith takes a hardline approach to immigration, opposing most proposals for an earned path to citizenship and supporting expanded use of detention and physical barriers. Smith favors policies that make it more difficult to seek asylum and has argued that Jordans reforms would constitute amnesty.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan supports ending mass incarceration for nonviolent offenses, eliminating discriminatory sentencing, and expanding reentry and restorative justice programs.
key_points: End mandatory minimums for nonviolent drug offenses and reform cash bail.
Expand diversion programs, treatment, and restorative justice initiatives.
Increase funding for public defenders and reentry services like housing and job training.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee believes the justice system should focus on accountability, rehabilitation, and prevention. Jordan supports ending mandatory minimum sentences for nonviolent drug offenses, reforming cash bail so people are not jailed simply for being poor, and expanding diversion and treatment options. The plan also calls for addressing racial disparities in sentencing and investing in robust reentry programs that provide housing, education, and job training.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith opposes major sentencing reforms and supports maintaining mandatory minimums and cash bail in most cases.
key_points: Supports mandatory minimum sentences for a wide range of offenses.
Opposes eliminating cash bail, citing public safety concerns.
Favors increased prison construction over diversion and reentry investments.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith argues that proposals like ending cash bail and reducing mandatory minimums will make communities less safe. Smith supports keeping or expanding mandatory minimum sentences and has opposed bills aimed at reducing incarceration for nonviolent offenses. Smith favors increased funding for prisons rather than diversion and reentry programs.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan strongly supports expanding voting access, ending partisan gerrymandering, and increasing transparency in campaign finance.
key_points: Restore and strengthen the Voting Rights Act and expand early voting and vote-by-mail.
Require independent redistricting commissions to end partisan gerrymandering.
Increase disclosure of political spending and support public financing of campaigns.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee believes that protecting democracy is foundational to every other issue. Jordan supports restoring and strengthening the Voting Rights Act, establishing national standards for early voting and vote-by-mail, and making Election Day a federal holiday. Jordan also backs independent redistricting commissions and stronger campaign finance disclosure rules so voters know who is funding political ads.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith supports restrictive voting measures and opposes independent redistricting commissions and most campaign finance reforms.
key_points: Backs strict voter ID laws and limits on mail-in and early voting.
Opposes independent redistricting commissions, defending partisan map-drawing.
Rejects expanded campaign finance disclosure and public financing proposals.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith has supported laws that make it harder to vote, including stricter ID requirements and reduced early voting periods. Smith opposes independent redistricting commissions and has defended partisan control over map-drawing. Smith also rejects stronger campaign finance disclosure rules, arguing they chill free speech, and opposes public financing of elections.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Jordan supports major investments in roads, bridges, transit, broadband, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
key_points: Prioritize repairing aging roads and bridges and modernizing water and sewer systems.
Expand public transit, bike lanes, and complete streets for all users.
Build climate-resilient infrastructure and expand broadband and EV charging networks.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Jordan Lee views infrastructure as both a jobs program and a quality-of-life issue. Jordan supports major federal investments to repair aging roads and bridges, modernize water and sewer systems, expand high-speed broadband, and build out clean transportation options like public transit, bike lanes, and EV charging. Climate resilience is a key priority, with funding directed toward strengthening infrastructure against floods, heat, and storms.

candidate_issue_positions.position_summary: Pat Smith supports limited, targeted infrastructure funding and opposes large federal climate-related infrastructure packages.
key_points: Supports basic road and bridge maintenance but opposes expansive federal infrastructure bills.
Skeptical of major federal spending on transit, bike lanes, and EV charging.
Opposes tying infrastructure funding to climate goals or labor standards.
candidate_issue_positions.full_text: Pat Smith favors a narrower approach to infrastructure, supporting basic maintenance of roads and bridges while opposing large federal packages that include transit, broadband, and climate resilience investments. Smith has voted against bills that tie infrastructure funding to climate targets or union labor standards, arguing they are too costly and restrictive.
